name: Dragon's Blood
description: Dragon's Blood is a vibrant red resin harvested from various plant species across the globe, most notably Dracaena, Daemonorops, and Croton trees. Its name evokes powerful imagery, connecting it to the mythical dragon and its potent life force. In numerous cultures, Dragon's Blood is revered for its protective, healing, and empowering properties. It is believed to amplify spells and rituals, banish negative energies, and attract love and prosperity. Shamans and healers traditionally utilize it for wound healing, stopping bleeding, and treating skin conditions. The deep red color is associated with blood, vitality, and courage, making it a symbol of strength and resilience. Its aroma is often described as earthy, spicy, and slightly sweet, contributing to its use in incense blends for spiritual cleansing and meditation. Dragon's Blood holds a significant place in folk magic, Wicca, and other spiritual practices. It is often incorporated into sachets, amulets, and talismans for protection and good fortune. The burning of Dragon's Blood incense is a common practice to purify spaces, ward off evil spirits, and enhance psychic abilities. Throughout history, it has been traded and valued for its medicinal and magical qualities, solidifying its status as a sacred and powerful herb across diverse cultures. The resin is more than just a substance; it is a conduit to ancient energies and a symbol of the dragon's enduring legacy. It represents the potent intersection of the natural and supernatural realms, embodying the life force and power associated with mythical dragons. Dragon's Blood is a reminder of the hidden energies within nature and the potential for harnessing them for healing, protection, and spiritual growth. Its continued use underscores its timeless appeal and enduring significance in various spiritual traditions.
subtitle: Resin of Power, Protection, and Vitality

keyMyths.title: The Origin of Dragon's Blood
keyMyths.description: Legend says Dragon's Blood sprung from the battle between a dragon and an elephant, where their mingled blood created the first tree. This tale signifies the herb's potent connection to life force and powerful energies.

sources.author: Scott Cunningham
